title: Here's Every Award SINNERS Won At The 2026 Oscars
text: Ryan Coogler's "Sinners" made history at the 2026 Oscars, winning four Academy Awards from a record-breaking 16 nominations. Among the victories was Best Original Screenplay, which went to Ryan Coogler. The vampire horror film, which became a breakthrough moment for the genre at the Academy Awards, also won Best Actor for Michael B. Jordan (who played dual roles as twin brothers), Best Cinematography for Autumn Durald Arkapaw (making her the first woman to win in this category), and Best Original Score for Ludwig Göransson. The film's dominance across multiple award categories showcases its cultural impact and technical excellence. With its record 16 nominations, "Sinners" surpassed the previous nomination record, making it the most nominated film in Oscar history. Coogler's win for Best Original Screenplay represents only the second time a Black writer has won in this category, following Jordan Peele's historic win for "Get Out" in 2017. The screenplay's success at the Oscars came after the film had already won major precursor awards at BAFTA, Critics Choice, and the Writers Guild Awards.
